metadata.title: The Ground is Shifting
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nik Bear Brown
metadata.credit_line: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. You put your work on GitHub with a careful README, but the ground is shifting under that signal. A README claims credit; commit signatures reveal how much of the complex logic is really yours. Authorship percentage measures what share of the hard logic actually carries your own commits. That is the difference between saying 'I built this' and 'I contributed to this.'

artifactLines: You put your work on GitHub with a careful README, but the ground is shifting under that signal.
A README claims credit; commit signatures reveal how much of the complex logic is really yours.
Authorship percentage measures what share of the hard logic actually carries your own commits.
That is the difference between saying 'I built this' and 'I contributed to this.'

beats.narration_text: The idea that a README says 'I built this' while commit signatures reveal 'I contributed to this' reframed how I see my portfolio. I want to audit myself. Help me: (1) define what authorship percentage on complex logic actually measures, (2) show how someone could game a green commit graph without owning the hard logic, and (3) list three signals a reviewer could trust instead of my own self-description.

props.command: The idea that a README says 'I built this' while commit signatures reveal 'I contributed to this' reframed how I see my portfolio. I want to audit myself. Help me: (1) define what authorship percentage on complex logic actually measures, (2) show how someone could game a green commit graph without owning the hard logic, and (3) list three signals a reviewer could trust instead of my own self-description.
output: You’ve spent three months building something you’re actually prou
The candidate who gets fifteen interviews

beats.narration_text: Written by Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI is an educational bridge program where Fellows learn experiential AI by doing AI — building real projects and shipping real research.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — Take the central idea from 'The Ground is Shifting' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each. Then… That's the prompt that actually tests whether you got it. Run it and see where it surprises you.

props.command: Take the central idea from 'The Ground is Shifting' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each. Then name the failure mode I'm most likely to hit.
